name: General Data Protection Regulation
description: EU regulation governing the collection, use, and storage of personal data of EU residents, with strict consent requirements and rights for data subjects.

name: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act
description: US federal law protecting the privacy and security of individually identifiable health information, requiring covered entities to implement safeguards.

name: California Consumer Privacy Act
description: California state law giving consumers the right to know about, delete, and opt out of the sale of their personal information collected by businesses.

name: System and Organization Controls 2
description: Auditing framework developed by AICPA for service organizations to demonstrate controls over security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy.

name: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
description: Global security standard for organizations that handle branded credit cards, requiring protection of cardholder data and secure network infrastructure.

name: ISO/IEC 27001
description: International standard specifying requirements for establishing, implementing, maintaining, and continually improving an information security management system.

name: Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act
description: US federal law protecting the privacy of student education records, giving parents and eligible students rights to access and control their records.

name: Children's Online Privacy Protection Act
description: US federal law imposing requirements on operators of websites or online services directed to children under 13, requiring parental consent for data collection.

name: Federal Trade Commission Act
description: US federal law prohibiting unfair or deceptive acts or practices in commerce, enforced by the FTC to protect consumers and promote competition.

name: Financial Industry Regulatory Authority Rules
description: Self-regulatory organization overseeing broker-dealers in the United States, setting rules for securities industry conduct and investor protection.

name: Basel III
description: International regulatory framework for banks developed by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision, addressing capital adequacy, stress testing, and liquidity risk.

name: Anti-Money Laundering and Know Your Customer
description: Regulations requiring financial institutions to verify client identities and monitor transactions to prevent money laundering and terrorist financing.

name: NIST Cybersecurity Framework
description: Voluntary framework developed by NIST providing standards, guidelines, and best practices for managing cybersecurity risk across critical infrastructure sectors.

name: Controlling the Assault of Non-Solicited Pornography and Marketing Act
description: US federal law establishing requirements for commercial email messages, giving recipients the right to stop receiving emails and specifying penalties for violations.

name: Telephone Consumer Protection Act
description: US federal law restricting telephone solicitations and the use of automated telephone equipment, regulating robocalls, autodialers, and unsolicited texts.

name: Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act
description: US federal law requiring financial institutions to explain how they share and protect customers' private information, with Safeguards Rule for data security.

name: Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act
description: Canadian federal privacy law governing how private sector organizations collect, use, and disclose personal information in the course of commercial activities.

name: Privacy Act 1988
description: Australian federal law regulating how Australian government agencies and private sector organizations with turnover over $3M handle personal information.

name: Personal Data Protection Act
description: Singapore law governing the collection, use, and disclosure of personal data by private organizations, with obligations for data protection and individual rights.

name: Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados
description: Brazilian data protection law regulating the processing of personal data of individuals in Brazil, with rights for data subjects similar to GDPR.
